steps.title: Identify the structures
steps.content: Two independent clauses need to be joined: Dickens's novel originally had a different title, though and while writing and editing, Dickens had planned to call the novel Mag's Diversions. The transition though caps the first clause.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content: Choice A uses title, though; — a comma sets off the trailing transition though, and the semicolon then joins the two independent clauses. Choice B uses a comma after though, creating a comma splice. Choices C and D drop the needed punctuation between the independent clauses. That matches choice A — A is correct.
